bead.title: Benchmark: axon backend vs JSONL on 1100-bead archive
bead.description: Add a benchmark that exercises ready/blocked/show queue ops on a 1100-bead corpus (current archive scale) for both jsonl and axon backends. Capture wall-time and allocs. Report parity-or-better as the axon adoption gate per parent AC6. Output benchmark results into the per-attempt evidence dir.
bead.acceptance: 1. Benchmark file under cli/internal/bead/ exercises ready/blocked/show on 1100-bead corpus. 2. Runs against both jsonl and axon backends. 3. Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl. 4. Benchmark is reproducible via go test -bench.

events.body: {"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}
{"attempt_id":"20260503T024909-67788006","harness":"claude","input_tokens":16,"output_tokens":3026,"total_tokens":3042,"cost_usd":0.44921875000000006,"duration_ms":70779,"exit_code":0}

This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend. The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely. Recommended follow-up: 1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5 (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands) 2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first. 3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends. Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.

no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: This bead requires benchmarking the axon backend against the JSONL backend on a 1100-bead corpus, but the axon backend has not yet been implemented. The implementing bead, ddx-95ec5ed5 ("Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"), is still status=open, and there is no axon_backend.go (or equivalent) in cli/internal/bead/ — the only Backend implementation present is backend_jsonl.go. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") are unsatisfiable without that backend.

The bead's dependency DAG was not encoded: per the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) decomposition, this benchmark bead was created with an implicit dependency on ddx-95ec5ed5, but no `ddx bead dep add` edge was recorded, so the queue surfaced this bead as ready prematurely.

Recommended follow-up:
  1. ddx bead dep add ddx-781a15cf ddx-95ec5ed5
     (record the missing edge so the queue stops surfacing this bead until the axon backend lands)
  2. Land ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface refactor, in_progress) and ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon implementation) first.
  3. Re-queue ddx-781a15cf; at that point the benchmark can be written meaningfully against both backends.

Writing a partial benchmark (JSONL only) would satisfy neither AC2 nor AC3 and would mislead the parent epic's AC6 ("parity or better than JSONL") gate.
